name: Access Services
description: Administers ADA complementary paratransit services for 46 fixed-route transit operators in Los Angeles County. Provides curb-to-curb, shared-ride transportation for people with disabilities who cannot use fixed-route public transit.

service_coverage.primary: Within 3/4 mile of fixed-route bus lines and METRO Rail stations in Los Angeles County
service_coverage.extensions: Extends into portions of San Bernardino, Orange, and Ventura counties served by LA County bus lines
service_coverage.exclusions: Does not serve commuter rail or commuter bus routes
service_coverage.geography: From Lancaster to Long Beach, Malibu to Montclair

service_hours.note: 24 hours in most Los Angeles County areas

board_appointed_by: City of Los Angeles Mayor's Office
LA County Independent Living Centers Collaboration

fleet.ownership: Fleet leased to regional providers at $1/month
fleet.note: Access generally provides the majority of vehicles needed by transportation service contractors

ctsa_functions.description: Created by Assembly Bill 120 in 1979 to coordinate social service transportation
activities: Information and technical assistance to LA County specialized transportation providers
Learning and Development Program for transit professionals
Participation in Metro LTSS and BOS planning meetings
Technical assistance for Section 5310, 5316 (JARC), and 5317 (New Freedom) grants
Coordinated Action Plan development for SCAG

emergency_preparedness.framework: National Incident Management System (NIMS) and Incident Command System (ICS)
emergency_preparedness.covid_response: Delivered over 430,000 meals during pandemic, facilitated vaccination transportation
